potential_technical_flaws: Missing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) header

fix_laws.fix: Implement X-XSS-Protection header with mode=block to prevent reflected cross-site scripting attacks.
Implement X-Content-Type-Options header with the value nosniff to mitigate MIME-sniffing attacks.
Implement Content-Security-Policy header to restrict the sources from which certain types of content can be loaded on the site.

fix_laws.flaw: Missing HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) header
fix_laws.fix: Implement HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) header to enforce secure connections to the site.
Implement X-Frame-Options header with value 'DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' to prevent Clickjacking attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is vulnerable to XSS attacks which can allow attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users.

fix.law: Implement input validation and encode user-supplied data before displaying it on web pages to prevent XSS attacks.

vulnerabilities.description: The website does not have a Content Security Policy (CSP) in place, making it more vulnerable to various types of attacks like XSS.

fix.law: Implement a strong Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate the risk of XSS attacks and other malicious activities.

vulnerabilities.description: The website is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks which can allow attackers to manipulate the database and access sensitive information.

fix.law: Use parameterized queries or stored procedures to prevent SQL injection attacks and sanitize user inputs.

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be vulnerable to IDOR attacks, where users can access unauthorized data or resources.

fix.law: Implement proper access controls and authorization checks to prevent IDOR attacks and restrict users' access to only authorized resources.

vulnerabilities.description: The website may be using outdated libraries or components with known vulnerabilities, increasing the risk of exploitation.

fix.law: Regularly update and patch all libraries and components used in the website to their latest secure versions to prevent known vulnerabilities exploitation.

fix.solution: Implement security headers like Content Security Policy (CSP), Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and X-XSS-Protection to enhance the security of the website.
fix.example: Add a Content-Security-Policy header with the appropriate directives to whitelist trusted sources for content such as scripts, stylesheets, and images.

fix.solution: Set secure flag and HTTPOnly flag for cookies to prevent them from being accessed over insecure channels and mitigate cross-site scripting attacks.
fix.example: Set the 'Secure' attribute on cookies to ensure they are only sent over HTTPS connections.

fix.solution: Regularly update all software, plugins, and libraries to the latest versions to patch known vulnerabilities.
fix.example: Enable automatic updates for CMS, plugins, and server software to ensure that security patches are applied promptly.

fix.solution: Disable directory listing to prevent unauthorized access and information disclosure.
fix.example: Configure the web server to return a 403 Forbidden error instead of listing the contents of directories without an index file.

fix.solution: Sanitize input data and use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection attacks.
fix.example: Validate and sanitize user inputs before constructing database queries to ensure that malicious SQL commands cannot be injected.
